metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Scenario signal

session-reset asserts: after a no-op turn the user issues the reset command, the old session row disappears, and a NEW session_id appears in ready. State topology ready → working → ready → ready → working → ready, final ready, ≤0 tool calls. The load-bearing question for the daemon is whether vibe's reset continues in the SAME session dir (like kiro's /clear → invisible to the daemon → incapable) or starts a NEW ~/.vibe/logs/session/<id>/ dir (like claudecode → observable). The answer is NEW dir.

## agent = yes

Vibe (live CLI v2.19.0, source under .../uv/tools/mistral-vibe/) has an in-REPL `/clear` slash command: `vibe/cli/commands.py:64-68` registers `clear` (alias `/clear`, "Clear conversation history", handler `_clear_history`). `vibe/cli/textual_ui/app.py:2685-2690` `_clear_history` calls `agent_loop.clear_history()`, which (`vibe/core/agent_loop.py:1919-1942`) persists the current messages then calls `_reset_session(keep_parent=False)`. `_reset_session` (agent_loop.py:1848-1857) mints a FRESH id via `generate_session_id(...)` and calls `session_logger.reset_session(new_id)`. `SessionLogger.reset_session` (core/session/session_logger.py:393-402) recomputes `self.session_dir = self.save_folder`, and `save_folder` (session_logger.py:61-72) builds `session_<UTC-timestamp>_<short-id>` — a brand-new directory. So `/clear` rotates to a NEW ~/.vibe/logs/session/ dir with a new session_id, NOT an in-place clear. keep_parent=False means no parent link is set (a clean new session, not a fork).

## daemon = full

The adapter keys the daemon session_id off the `session_<...>` directory name (`sessionIDFromPath`, adapter.go:56-65). A new dir therefore surfaces as a genuinely new session_id — `new_session:true`. The three spec bullets map to observable events:
1. New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id → the FilesUnderRoot watcher detects the new dir's messages.jsonl and mints a fresh session (transcript_new), same mechanism as claudecode's /clear UUID rotation.
2. Old session disappears → both vibe sessions share ONE vibe process (same PID, same cwd); `DiscoverPID` (pid.go) binds the new session to that same PID, and `PIDManager.cleanupStalePIDHolders` (core/application/services/pid_manager.go:566-576) emits `KindTranscriptRemoved` for the prior same-PID session — the generic, matcher-agnostic #169 cleanup path claudecode's session-reset relies on. The old dir persists on disk; the daemon drops its row.
3. Subsequent turn drives the NEW session ready→working→ready normally — standard turn arc on the new dir.
None of the daemon's known vibe gaps (no per-turn cost; no context-WINDOW resolution since mistral models are absent from core/pkg/capacity; per-event timestamps backfilled to scan time; no Control/backchannel; no subagent linking) touch any session-reset bullet — they are caveats, not spec-required observables here.

## driver = gap:wait_turn

The reset itself is NOT a driver gap: `/clear` is an in-REPL slash command, so it is a `slash` step, and `slash` IS in the driver's elicited set (driver-interactive.sh:113 `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"`). The false reset_session/keys gap is ruled out. The genuine gap is `wait_turn`: the lifecycle recipe (send → wait_turn → /clear → send → wait_turn) needs turn-completion detection, but SEAM 2 `wait_turn()` (driver-interactive.sh:168-170) dispatches to `not_implemented` (exit 3) and is absent from DRIVE_ELICITS — so recipe-lint raises a semantic_gap before recording. This matches every sibling vibe lifecycle cell (session-start, long-idle, model-context-display all assessed driver=gap:wait_turn). Two further scaffold seams block recording of ANY vibe cell and are ported alongside by record: SEAM 1 — the launch (line 155) execs `"mistral-vibe"`, not on PATH (only `vibe` is); SEAM 3 — `launch_repl` (line 158) never resolves/stores the transcript path into `SES_TRANSCRIPT[$ACTIVE]`, so `emit_session_contract` (line 210) records nothing to verify, and after /clear the driver must re-resolve SES_TRANSCRIPT[$ACTIVE] to the NEW dir. This is tooling work, not an observability limit — the cell keeps its recipe + spec.
caveats: Provisional on live drive: daemon=full is derived from reading the adapter + shared PIDManager cleanup, not a vibe recording. The transcript_removed timing and the new-session appearance ORDERING are emission properties record must settle. In particular, clear_history persists the OLD session's messages.jsonl first, then _reset_session mints the new dir whose messages.jsonl may not materialize until the SECOND turn saves — so the new session may not surface (and the old may not be cleaned) until prompt 2 completes. The spec's max_delay bounds are generous to accommodate this; record tightens them from the recording.

keep_parent=False on /clear means the new session carries NO parent_session_id — correct for a reset (a clean new session, not a fork). vibe's /resume-based fork path (fork(), agent_loop.py:1861) DOES set a parent, but that is a different scenario.
The daemon does not emit per-turn cost for vibe (the sidecar retains only last-turn tokens, not backfillable), so the spec asserts tokens_nonzero + model but NOT cost.
model mistral-medium-3.5 displays but its context WINDOW is unresolved (mistral models absent from core/pkg/capacity); irrelevant to session-reset (no context bar asserted).
messages.jsonl carries no per-event timestamp; backfill uses scan time — irrelevant to the state-topology assertions here.

LIVE-DRIVE FINDING (record, 2 deterministic runs): the daemon surfaces ONLY the post-/clear session as a real session_<id>. The INITIAL session dir stays a presession (proc-<pid>) and is transcript_removed WITHOUT promoting to its real session_<id> — its ready->working->ready "ok" turn arc is never captured. A single-session vibe recording (basic-turn, session-start) DOES promote presession->real, so the /clear rotation is what strands the initial presession. Recording commits as VALIDATION DRIFT (5/9 phases: v2_session_birth onward unmet because v1 matched the second dir); spec expected.jsonl left intact for maintainer review. Increasing the pre-/clear sleep to 10s did NOT change it (structural, not timing).

sources.note: Lines 64-68: `clear` command (alias /clear) → handler _clear_history. Confirms /clear is an in-REPL slash command (→ a `slash` driver step, not reset_session).
clear_history (1919-1942) → _reset_session(keep_parent=False); _reset_session (1848-1857) mints a fresh generate_session_id and calls session_logger.reset_session — a NEW session, no parent link.

reset_session (393-402) recomputes session_dir = save_folder; save_folder (61-72) builds session_<UTC-timestamp>_<short-id> — a BRAND-NEW dir. So /clear rotates to a new ~/.vibe/logs/session/<id>/ dir (new-dir, like claudecode; NOT in-place like kiro).
sessionIDFromPath (56-65) derives the daemon session_id from the session_<...> dir name — a new dir is a new session_id (new_session:true).

cleanupStalePIDHolders (566-576) emits KindTranscriptRemoved for prior same-PID sessions when the new session binds the same vibe PID — the generic #169 'old session disappears' mechanism, matcher-kind agnostic.

Line 113 DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" (slash IS elicited → /clear ok, not a reset gap). Line 168-170 wait_turn()->not_implemented (the real gap). Line 155 launch execs "mistral-vibe" not on PATH; line 158 SEAM 3 transcript-path capture unported.

preconditions: vibe CLI on PATH (~/.local/bin/vibe, v2.19.0) and authenticated (Mistral API key)
irrlichd is recording (run-cell.sh isolated mode, or --attach against a --record daemon)

driver seams ported by record: SEAM 1 launch binary vibe (not mistral-vibe), SEAM 2 wait_turn poll on messages.jsonl, SEAM 3 transcript-path capture AND re-resolution of SES_TRANSCRIPT[$ACTIVE] to the NEW dir after /clear
setup: Create a fresh empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ so the session's working_directory is unique
Vibe mints its own session_<ts>_<hash> id on launch; the daemon session_id is that directory name
/clear mints a SECOND session_<ts2>_<hash2> dir under the same cwd/PID — the driver re-points subsequent turns at it (SEAM 3)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: fresh

verify: events.jsonl shows the FIRST session_<ts>_<hash> entering ready, then a working/ready cycle for turn 1
after /clear, events.jsonl emits transcript_removed for the FIRST session (same-PID cleanup) — the 'old session disappears' assertion
events.jsonl contains a DIFFERENT session_<ts2>_<hash2> appearing in ready after /clear (new session_id, fresh row, new_session:true)
the second turn's working/ready cycle is on the NEW session dir, not the old one
meta.json sidecar supplies model mistral-medium-3.5 and a non-zero context_tokens on turn_done
